channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: League of Legends Voice Chat Confirmed But Not Any Time Soon
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has confirmed that voice chat is coming to League of Legends, but it may not be for quite some time.

Dataminers discovered files for LoL voice chat on the PBE earlier this year, yet Riot had not officially commented until now.

The publisher has released a dev blog revealing that team-based voice comms will indeed be added to the game, but not until developers believe the feature is in a good place with a lower risk of toxicity.

Riot claims: “We think when done well, team voice chat opens up the doors for enhanced teamwork and coordination, and has the potential to improve the overall gameplay experience for players that would like to use it.”

The blog adds: “We’ll do this by working with you all to find the best version of team voice chat for the League community.”

Riot Addresses LoL Voice Chat Concerns

Riot Games has been working hard to combat the toxic reputation of the game and has been cracking down on bad behaviour for a while now.

Despite this, the publisher seemingly doesn’t think that LoL is ready for voice chat yet as the dev blog states: “We want to get this right, so it isn’t going to happen fast.”

Riot claims it hadn’t added voice chat sooner because it didn’t meet the publisher’s standards for safety and reliability, while now technology has advanced enough to “identify bad actors.”

Riot acknowledged concerns over voice chat toxicity, stating:

“We hear that you are worried about a world where some people use Team Voice to be jerks, but we want to reassure you: we’re listening to your concerns and we are committed to making sure that that world does not come to pass.”

The dev blog claims that developers will meet with players around the world to test the new feature and suggests it may roll out one language or region at a time.

Currently, Riot states there is no timeline as to when voice chat will be added, but says more details will be shared in another dev blog later this year.

Do you think voice chat will be good for LoL or make it even more toxic? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

The publishers are being kept busy as LoL, TFT and other Riot titles have been banned for under-18s in Brazil.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Multiple Riot Games Titles Banned for Under 18s in Brazil
item.content:encoded:
Riot Games has announced that the majority of its games are changing age ratings to 18+ in order to comply with new laws in Brazil.

The Statute of Children and Adolescents (ECA) will come into effect in the South American country on Tuesday, March 17, with age verification and parental control requirements being enforced.

Riot Games Titles Have Age Rating Change in Brazil

From Monday, March 16, League of Legends, TeamFight Tactics, Wild Rift, 2XKO, and Legends of Runeterra will all be rated as 18+ games in Brazil.

Meanwhile, Valorant players aged between 12 and 17 will need parental consent in order to access the first-person shooter.

Players of these 18+ games in Brazil will have to verify their age before playing with either a CPF number (tax identifier), payment card (debit or credit card), scan of an identity document, or via an age estimation facial scan.

Valorant fans aged under 18 will need to provide an email address of their father, mother, or legal guardian, who will then have to give permission through Riot’s parental control portal.

However, on the Riot Game website, the publisher claims it expects that these games will have their age ratings return as soon as next year:

“We will continue to evaluate these games and their features under the requirements of the ECA and expect them to return to their original age ratings by early 2027.”

Underage Gamers in Brazil Will Lose Access to Riot Games Accounts

From March 18, Riot Games accounts belonging to underage players, who were honest about their age, will be blocked and put on pause for games that have had their rating changed.

However, these players won’t lose their accounts, the progress they’ve made, or any cosmetics they’ve bought. Riot claims these accounts will be put on pause securely and will return to normal once the player is allowed to access them again.

The publisher then stated again that it aims to return the age ratings of its games back to their original status in early 2027. At that point, players with blocked accounts will need parental permission to play again.

Do you think these stricter age ratings for video games are a good thing? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And check out the latest news with Riot Games’ titles, including the leaked theme for the next LoL season.
